photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: brown brick with red/yellow Chinese community signage at upper level
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: 2-storey brown brick commercial building; ground floor LEFT: KING OF KENSINGTON vintage clothing store with open storefront, clothing racks on sidewalk, graffiti-covered walls; ground floor RIGHT: brown brick, dark glass door with gold Chinese characters '大鹏' above, security grille; upper floor: large red/yellow sign '加拿大安省海南同鄉會 / THE HAINAN ASSOCIATION OF ONTARIO CANADA / 217 AUGUSTA AVE / HAINAN COMMUNITY CENTRE / TEL. 416-346-7477'; brown brick throughout, concrete party wall visible on right
photo_observations.window_details: ground floor: 2 storefront openings (King of Kensington open storefront + dark glass door with security grille); upper floor: sign covers entire upper facade, no visible windows
photo_observations.door_details: two entries: one for King of Kensington store (open storefront), one dark glass door with gold Chinese characters '大鹏' and security grille for Hainan Association
photo_observations.cornice_details: sign band substitutes for any traditional cornice
photo_observations.decorative_details: large red/yellow association sign covering upper facade; gold Chinese characters '大鹏' above dark glass door; concrete party wall on right
photo_observations.storefront_description: dual-unit ground floor: KING OF KENSINGTON vintage clothing store (open storefront with clothing racks on sidewalk, graffiti-covered walls) and Hainan Association entry (dark glass door with security grille)
photo_observations.signage_observed: 加拿大安省海南同鄉會 / THE HAINAN ASSOCIATION OF ONTARIO CANADA / 217 AUGUSTA AVE / HAINAN COMMUNITY CENTRE / TEL. 416-346-7477; KING OF KENSINGTON
photo_observations.alterations_visible: multi-tenant storefront; King of Kensington open storefront with graffiti-covered walls; large association sign covers upper facade; concrete party wall visible on right

facade_description: 217 Augusta Ave presents a converted house-form, detached facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its converted house-form, detached typology. The facade reads primarily as brick, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 0 window bays from lower to upper levels, using storefront, and 2 principal entrances. Character-defining facade elements include cornice. HCD note: The property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles.
